channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Convicted Fyre Fest fraudster Billy McFarland's return to Mexico 'sounds like a ticking time bomb': experts
item.description: Fyre Fest 2 is coming to Mexico in late May through early June after founder Billy McFarland announced the return of the music event that failed in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal workers start to get a new email demanding their accomplishments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees are starting to receive another email requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments, a renewed attempt by President Donald Trump and billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk to demand answers from the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison Friday, saying there were enough questions about his guilt that she could not move forward with his execution.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas GOP leaders defend appointee after tense exchange over diversity hiring
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — An appointee of Texas Gov. Greg Abbott wiped away tears and gave an emotional defense of her credentials after a tense exchange over diversity hiring policies, which was later followed by the state's top Republicans rushing to support her publicly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Bryan Kohberger transcript, Princeton preppy murder, Gabby Petito's last days
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Haitian man charged in NC triple murder flew into US under Biden migrant flights program: ICE
item.description: ICE said a Haitian migrant, who had flown into the US as part of former President Biden's migrant flight program, was arrested and charged with triple murder in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's transportation department cancels new review step that could've slowed state projects
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is backing off its demand for an extra layer of federal scrutiny whenever states seek even minor changes to their transportation plans, after the rescinded requirement sparked concern that some payments for roads, bridges and transit would be de
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: To her, Hurricane Helene debris isn’t trash. It is full of memories
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — The tops of dried, bent cornstalks crunch underfoot. Jill Holtz’s gaze is fixed on the ground ahead. She wanders into the nearby woods and weaves between twisted branches. Then, Holtz spots something and starts to riffle through the withered twigs.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, ABC 4

item.title: Savage timeline in Princeton preppy murder could bolster insanity defense for suspect brother: expert
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man known as a star student in high school who is now accused of killing his brother likely had a mental breakdown, an expert said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles invites Trump to UK for a state visit
item.description: King Charles III has invited President Trump for a state visit to the United Kingdom, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the president during their meeting Thursday in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Wendy Williams: 'I need a new attorney’ before cognitive test
item.description: Williams said her conservatorship feels "suffocating," comparing it to prison, and suspects her guardian may be misusing her money.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Florida teen tries vandalizing ex's car, ends up painting neighbor's vehicle instead
item.description: The 18-year-old admitted she was upset with her ex-boyfriend and had decided to throw eggs at and spray-paint his vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: Investigators have been searching for Campbell since he vanished in 1983 while out on bond and facing charges of attempted first degree murder.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Julio Foolio murder fights solitary confinement as prosecutors warns of witness threats
item.description: One of the men accused in the Julio Foolio murder case was back in court Thursday, this time focusing on his conditions in solitary confinement.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman suspected in Colorado Tesla dealership vandalism charged in federal court
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Federal prosecutors have charged a woman in a string of vandalism against a Colorado Tesla dealership, including throwing Molotov cocktails at vehicles and spray painting “Nazi cars” on the building along with a message that appeared directed at company co-founder Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WGN 9

item.title: Man on 'Most Wanted' list for over 4 decades caught in New Mexico
item.description: After more than four decades, Stephen Craig Campbell, one of the United States' most wanted men, is now behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Bela Olague, KRON4

item.title: As Trump's deadline to eliminate DEI nears, few schools openly rush to make changes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Schools and colleges across the U.S. face a Friday deadline to end diversity programs or risk having their federal money pulled by the Trump administration, yet few are openly rushing to make changes.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, JOCELYN GECKER and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Betsy Arakawa, concert pianist married to actor Gene Hackman, dead at 65
item.description: Betsy Arakawa, a concert pianist and co-founder of a home furnishing business, was found dead Wednesday in her Santa Fe, New Mexico, home along with actor-husband Gene Hackman and their dog, according to authorities. She was 65.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Navy sailor pleads guilty in plot to attack Naval Station Great Lakes, allegedly on behalf of Iranian group, feds say
item.description: NORTH CHICAGO — A former U.S. Navy sailor faces two decades in prison after pleading guilty to plotting to attack Naval Station Great Lakes in North Chicago, allegedly on behalf of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps in order to avenge the death of one of its leaders.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP Sen. Mike Lee suggests letting ‘pirates’ take on cartels on the border
item.description: He floated the idea that Congress and President Trump should allow private citizens, or "pirates," to take on international cartels to protect national security.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Republican lawmakers look for ways to weaken state judges
item.description: TOPEKA, Kansas (AP) — Measures in several state legislatures this year have called for new approaches to weaken the power of judges. One would abandon a centuries-old precedent that courts can decide whether laws are constitutional. Another would change how judges are selected.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina man set to face a firing squad seeks postponement, questioning execution procedures
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who is scheduled to die next week by firing squad is again asking that his execution be postponed and prison officials release more information about the state's lethal injection drug and procedures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homicide fugitive from Washington state arrested in Utah by FBI task force
item.description: A 20-year-old homicide fugitive was arrested in Utah for the January death of a high school student in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
